experiments.description: Baseline SAC+HER, unmodified. 256x3 MLP, batch 256, buffer 200k, HER k=4 future, STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING=1000, UPDATE_EVERY=1, N_UPDATES=1.
experiments.hypothesis: Baseline establishes the reference point. Expect low success_rate because the 10s budget combined with STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING=1000 leaves very few gradient updates available for SAC to actually learn anything.
experiments.vlm_feedback_summary: Robot ignores the goal entirely; arm collapses downward into the table across all 3 episodes. In ep1 the gripper actually starts within the success threshold of the goal but the deterministic policy actively walks AWAY from it. Policy is effectively untrained.
vlm_failure_modes: walks away from goal (no goal-tracking behavior)
arm collapses into table (strong downward bias in policy output)
ignores desired_goal portion of obs (similar end pose across different goals)
fails to exploit favorable starts (ep1 starts at goal, policy still leaves)
vlm_suggestions: STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING 1000 -> 100 (single highest-leverage change, doubles available gradient updates)
N_UPDATES 1 -> 4 (REDQ-style UTD, multiplies effective gradient signal)
dense reward shaping in training (-distance instead of sparse -1/0) for stronger gradient signal
smaller network (128x2 or 64x2) since obs is only 13-dim
experiments.lesson_learned: On a 10s wall-clock budget, the STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING=1000 random warmup consumes ~54% of total env steps before any gradient update fires. Only 853 SAC updates total, far too few to train a 410k-param net even on FetchReach. The dominant constraint here is gradient steps, not env experience.

insights: On a 10s budget, gradient-step budget (not env-step budget) is the dominant constraint. STEPS_BEFORE_LEARNING=1000 directly steals ~half of available updates.
promising_directions: Raise UTD ratio (N_UPDATES) to 4-8 with REDQ/DroQ-style critic regularization if needed
Dense reward shaping in training (eval reward stays sparse via env)
Smaller network for the 13-dim obs / 4-dim action FetchReach problem
